title: verbascum niveum subsp. pannosiforme
intro: Verbascum niveum subsp. pannosiforme, also known as the woolly mullein, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mullein genus, characterized by its large, white, or yellowish flowers and woolly leaves. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Verbascum niveum subsp. pannosiforme include its ability to soothe respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical creams and salves for its medicinal properties.
